sources.title: Scarlet Street
sources.description: German director Fritz Lang is best known for the highly influential films from relatively early in his career, especially Metropolis (1927) and M (1931). But he also had a brief Hollywood heyday during the mid 1940s, when he made some of his best films. Scarlet Street has been claimed by film noir. This attribution is understandable. The lighting is dark, with heavy use of shadows. The characters are all shady as well. Kitty is certainly a femme fatale, although hints are dropped that she is an inherently decent person who has become completely corrupted by Johnny. Being the most naive, Robinson is also the most sympathetic. But even he eventually turns to crime to fulfill his passions. But while Scarlet Street has elements of film noir, it is in reality a black comedy. While Robinson plays it straight throughout, Bennett and Duryea camp it up marvelously. Bennett laughs when Robinson claims to be a painter: "And here I had you pegged as a cashier!" She also has to suppress laughter when Robinson reveals that he is married, and shock when Duryea passes her off as the mysterious painter.

sources.title: Scarlet Street (1945)
sources.description: Cashier and part-time starving artist Christopher Cross is absolutely smitten with the beautiful Kitty March. Kitty plays along, but she's really only interested in Johnny, a two-bit crook. When Kitty and Johnny find out that art dealers are interested in Chris's work, they con him into letting Kitty take credit for the paintings. Cross allows it because he is in love with Kitty, but his love will only let her get away with so much. Watch on RetroFlix:  https://retroflix.org/scarlet-street-1945/

sources.title: Scarlet Street (1945)
sources.description: Scarlet Street (1945) is a film noir directed by Fritz Lang, based on the 1931 French film "La Chienne." The story revolves around Christopher Cross , a middle-aged, timid cashier who becomes infatuated with a young woman named Kitty . After crossing paths with Kitty, who is actually a con artist, Christopher unwittingly becomes embroiled in a web of deception. He initially helps support Kitty and her boyfriend Johnny , who sees an opportunity to exploit Christopher's affection for Kitty. When Christopher discovers that their relationship is purely a manipulation, chaos ensues, leading to a series of tragic events.

sources.title: Scarlet Street
sources.description: "Sultry woman's boyfriend has her hook middle-aged clerk for his money, leading first to ironic and then tragic complications. Second of a pair of closely related middle class nightmares directed by Fritz Lang. (Remake of Jean Renoir's 1931 French film, La Chienne.)" - noir expert Spencer Selby

sources.title: Oscar Nominee: Her allure always ends the same way - in murder! Scarlet Street (1945, Noir)
sources.description:
“It’s not murder… it’s art.”
Edward G. Robinson crushes it as Chris Cross – a meek cashier turned tortured artist. Joan Bennett sizzles as Kitty – a femme fatale who’d steal your soul between cigarettes. Dan Duryea oozes slime as Johnny, the manipulative boyfriend. And Fritz Lang (M, The Big Heat) directs this brutal descent into obsession, fraud, and murder with razor-sharp precision.

Original title: Scarlet Street (1945)

Black & White version: https://youtu.be/D_813YbAj3Y

🎬 CREDITS:
Directed by Fritz Lang
Screenplay by Dudley Nichols
Based on the novel La Chienne by Georges de La Fouchardière

⭐️ STARRING:
Edward G. Robinson - Christopher "Chris" Cross
Joan Bennett - Katherine "Kitty" March
Dan Duryea - Johnny Prince
Margaret Lindsay - Millie Ray
Rosalind Ivan - Adele Cross
Jess Barker - Damon Janeway
Charles Kemper - Patch-eye Higgins
Anita Sharp-Bolster - Mrs. Michaels 
Samuel S. Hinds - Charles Pringle
Vladimir Sokoloff - Pop LeJon
Arthur Loft - Delarowe
Russell Hicks - J.J. Hogarth

🔥 WHY IT’S ICONIC:
• Oscar Nominee: 1946’s boundary-pushing noir that dared to dissect toxic desire.
• Visually Reborn: Lang’s shadow-play in vivid HD color – every frame drips with doom.
• Twisted & Timeless: A cautionary tale that bites harder than cheap whiskey.

🌎 MULTI-LINGUAL:
SUBTITLES: 50+ LANGUAGES available! Click [CC] and select your language.
AUDIO: English, Italian & Spanish dubbed audio tracks

sources.title: SCARLET STREET video quality upgrade
sources.description: This print is much sharper and cleaner than the existing IA copies, and with a smaller download file. Fritz Lang's ironic film noir of a middle-age man's obsessive involvement with an alluring young woman. Strong performances by Bennett, Duryea and especially Edward G Robinson. Inspired by Jean Renoir's 1931 film "La Chienne." (Not related to D W Griffith's 1919 film "Scarlet Street.") Directed by Fritz Lang. Released in 1945. Complete print. TRIVIA; The twelve paintings John Decker made for this film were later exhibited at the Museum of Modern Art.

sources.title: Fritz Lang: A Commentary on Scarlet Street (1946)
MALA MUJER - (1945) [VOSE]
sources.description: Título original: Scarlet Street Se estrenó en los Estados Unidos el 28 de diciembre de 1945. En México el 30 de mayo de 1946, en el cine Alameda del D.F. (de 3,237 localidades) donde se mantuvo por dos semanas en cartelera. Duración: 97 min. País de origen: Los Estados Unidos de Norteamérica Dirección: Fritz Lang Guion: Dudley Nichols. Novela: Georges de La Fouchardière, André Mouézy-Éon Música: Hans J. Salter Fotografía: Milton R. Krasner (B&W) Reparto Edward G. Robinson, Joan Bennett, Dan Duryea, Jess Baker, Margaret Lindsay, Rosalind Ivan, Samuel S. Hinds, Vladimir Sokoloff, Anita Bolster, Charles Kemper, Russell Hicks, Arthur Loft Género: Film-Noir, Cine negro, Drama, Remake, Pintura, Años 30, Crimen, Thriller, Sinopsis Nueva York, año 1934. Christopher Cross es un simple cajero, infelizmente casado, cuya única pasión es la pintura. Una noche conoce a Kitty March, una atractiva buscavidas de la que se enamora y le hace creer que es un pintor de éxito. La chica y su novio Johnny, un tipo sin escrúpulos, aprovechan la ocasión para intentar explotar al pobre hombre, pues creen que sus cuadros valen mucho dinero. (FILMAFFINITY)

sources.title: Scarlet Street (Fritz Lang, 1945) Her lies. His masterpiece - Film-noir femme fatale trap!
sources.description:
Mild-mannered Chris Cross (Edward G. Robinson) paints himself into a corner when femme fatale Kitty March (Joan Bennett) and her slick accomplice Johnny Prince (Dan Duryea) spin his artistic dreams into a web of cash and deception. As Chris’s brushstrokes turn desperate, this canvas of con-artistry cracks—leaving Johnny framed and Chris haunted by a masterpiece gone rotten!

Colorized version: https://youtu.be/11WPnMv03p0

Screenwriter: Dudley Nichols

sources.title: Scarlet Street (remastered edition) (1945, Dir: Fritz Lang, noir, imdb score: 7.8)
sources.description: Scarlet Street is a 1945 American film noir directed by Fritz Lang. The screenplay concerns two criminals who take advantage of a middle-aged painter in order to steal his artwork. The film is based on the French novel La Chienne (literally The Bitch) by Georges de La Fouchardière, that previously had been dramatized on stage by André Mouëzy-Éon, and cinematically as La Chienne (1931) by director Jean Renoir. The principal actors Edward G. Robinson, Joan Bennett and Dan Duryea had earlier appeared together in The Woman in the Window (1944), also directed by Fritz Lang. Local authorities in three cities (New York, Milwaukee, and Atlanta) banned Scarlet Street early in 1946 because of its dark plot and themes.

sources.title: Scarlet Street (restored, with intro and outro) (1945, Dir: Fritz Lang, film noir, imdb score: 7.8)
sources.description: Scarlet Street is a 1945 American film noir directed by Fritz Lang . The screenplay concerns two criminals who take advantage of a middle-aged painter in order to steal his artwork. The film is based on the French novel La Chienne (literally The Bitch ) by Georges de La Fouchardière , which had been previously dramatized on stage by André Mouëzy-Éon , and cinematically as La Chienne (1931) by director Jean Renoir . The principal actors Edward G. Robinson , Joan Bennett and Dan Duryea had earlier appeared together in The Woman in the Window (1944), also directed by Lang. Local authorities in New York, Milwaukee, and Atlanta banned Scarlet Street early in 1946 because of its dark plot and themes. The film is in the public domain .

sources.title: Scarlet Street with subtitles
sources.description: This is a copy of the subtitled version of Fritz Lang's "Scarlet Street" published in youtube.com/watch?v=tHv3Jico_x4 . It is republished here because some people are barred from accessing YouTube. As the unsubtitled copy of the same film in archive.org/details/ScarletStreet is published with a dedication to the Public Domain, I am using the same dedication here.
